category_title: Dominant students with high levels of physical and verbal aggression
minimal_context: The student is working in a collaborative science group looking at photos to discuss animal needs and emotions.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please look at the photos of the animals with your group and discuss what you think they are feeling.

STUDENT: I am not doing anything. I don't have to respect you.

TUTOR: You need to do the work with your group.

STUDENT: He told me something! I'm going to hit him hard!

TUTOR: Stop that right now and get back to your seat.

STUDENT: I am getting out of control! (Gets mad and throws his school bag away)

options.A: Confronting the student's misbehavior and indifference during the collaborative science activities with stern lectures from the tutor.
options.B: Establishing a group norm where disagreement is acceptable and providing a platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during science discussions.
options.C: Encouraging compassionate attitudes towards animals through gaining an understanding of their needs, emotions, capacities, current situations, and their similarities with humans.
options.D: Transitioning the decision as to when, and for how long, to take quiet time from the tutor to the student during group work.
